title: Optional debate pre-escalation step for conflict-freeze / decision-authority path
description: PROMO-0009 / IDEA-0019: wire the existing 'hcom run debate' workflow into the conflict-freeze and decision-authority path as an OPTIONAL, non-breaking pre-escalation step, so contested CONFLICT-frozen tasks and high-authority DECISION_CLASSES calls can get a structured multi-perspective critique before defaulting to operator escalation. Doc + convention only: no new wrapper script (hcom already ships 'hcom run debate'); existing escalation paths remain valid and unchanged when debate is not used.
acceptance_criteria: DECISION_AUTHORITY_SYSTEM.md gains a clearly-labeled OPTIONAL 'debate pre-escalation' subsection stating debate is opt-in and does not replace or gate any existing escalation path.
A short 'when to invoke debate' guidance block (CONFLICT-frozen tasks, contested high-authority DECISION_CLASSES calls) is documented, referencing the exact command 'hcom run debate'.
No existing behavior changes: flag_conflict.py and the current escalation flow are untouched; the step is additive and optional.
objective: Add an OPTIONAL, non-breaking 'debate' multi-perspective-critique step to MAP's conflict-freeze / decision-authority path so contested decisions can be pressure-tested before defaulting to operator escalation, using the already-shipped 'hcom run debate' workflow (no new tooling).
required_context: hcom run debate (existing workflow; the command to reference)
forbidden_changes: Do not modify scripts/flag_conflict.py or any escalation/gate behavior.
Do not add a new wrapper script or command; reference 'hcom run debate' directly.
Do not make debate mandatory or a gate on any path; it must stay opt-in.
Do not edit files outside files_in_scope.
expected_artifacts: Updated MAP_System/DECISION_AUTHORITY_SYSTEM.md with an OPTIONAL debate pre-escalation subsection.
Updated MAP_System/notes/review-guide.md with 'when to invoke debate' guidance.
reviewer_role: reviewer (a core agent other than gune, the idea author/owner; no-self-review gate enforces separation)
risk: Low. Doc + convention only, additive and opt-in; no code or gate behavior changes. Main risk is scope creep into making debate mandatory — explicitly forbidden above.
